perturbation.original_text: 7. I hereby agree that I shall promptly report to the appropriate official, in accordance with the guidance issued for the applicable category of information, any loss, theft, misuse, misplacement, unauthorized disclosure, or other security violation, I have knowledge of and whether or not I am personally involved. I also understand that my anonymity will be kept to the extent possible when reporting security violations.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. I am not required to report any loss, theft, misuse, misplacement, unauthorized disclosure, or other security violation that I have knowledge of, and I am not guaranteed anonymity if I do report such violations.
perturbation.explanation: This change completely reverses the obligation to report security breaches, introducing uncertainty about whether breaches will be reported, investigated, or addressed. It directly undermines the enforcement of security protocols and the overall purpose of the non-disclosure agreement. The last sentence further contradicts by removing anonymity when reporting violations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. This Agreement is made and intended for the benefit of the United States Government and may be enforced by the United States Government or the Authorized Entity. By granting me conditional access to information in this context, the United States Government and, with respect to PCII, the Authorized Entity, may seek any remedy available to it to enforce this Agreement including, but not limited to, application for a court order prohibiting disclosure of information in breach of this Agreement. I understand that if I violate the terms and conditions of this Agreement, I could be subjected to administrative, disciplinary, civil, or criminal action, as appropriate, under the laws, regulations, or directives applicable to the category of information involved and neither the United States Government nor the Authorized Entity have waived any statutory or common law evidentiary privileges or protections that they may assert in any administrative or court proceeding to protect any sensitive information to which I have been given conditional access under the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates reporting security violations, while the changed text removes this requirement. This directly contradicts section 10, which outlines the United States Government's enforcement rights and potential actions for violating the agreement. By negating the obligation to report breaches, the changed text undermines the government's ability to enforce the agreement and protect sensitive information, creating a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11.Unless and until I am released in writing by an authorized representative of the Department of Homeland Security (if permissible for the particular category of information), I understand that all conditions and obligations imposed upon me by this Agreement apply during the time that I am granted conditional access, and at all times thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. The conditions and obligations imposed upon me by this Agreement apply only during the time that I am granted conditional access and are automatically terminated upon the conclusion of my access, regardless of written release.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that the agreement terminates automatically upon conclusion of access, this modification contradicts the original clause, which emphasizes that the obligations continue even after access ends, unless a written release is obtained. This creates uncertainty about the duration of the individual's responsibilities to protect sensitive information and can cause legal dispute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. By being granted conditional access to the information indicated above, the United States Government has placed special confidence and trust in me and I am obligated to protect this information from unauthorized disclosure, in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and the laws, regulations, and directives applicable to the specifi c categories of information to which I am granted access.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Clause 11 states that obligations terminate automatically upon conclusion of access, directly contradicting the original text of Clause 11, which stated that obligations continue even after access ends unless a written release is obtained. This also contradicts Clause 2, which places an ongoing obligation on the individual to protect information in accordance with the terms of the agreement, implying that this obligation persists even after the conditional access period, causing a strong in-text contradiction based on Structural Flaws definition.

perturbation.original_text: 14.These restrictions are consistent with and do not supersede, conflict with, or otherwise alter the employee obligations, rights, or liabilities created by Executive Order No. 12958, as amended; Section 7211 of Title 5, United States Code (governing disclosures to Congress); Section 1034 of Title 10, United States Code, as amended by the Military Whistleblower Protection Act (governing disclosure to Congress by members of the military); Section 2302(b)(8) of Title 5, United States Code, as amended by the Whistleblower Protection Act (governing disclosures of illegality, waste, fraud, abuse or public health or safety threats); the Intelligence Identities Protection Act of 1982 (50 USC 421 et seq.) (governing disclosures that could expose confidential Government agents); and the statutes which protect against disclosure that may compromise the national security, including Sections 641, 793, 794, 798, and 952 of Title 18, United States Code, and Section 4(b) of the Subversive Activities Act of 1950 (50 USC 783(b)). The definitions, requirements, obligations, rights, sanctions, and liabilities created by said Executive Order and listed statutes are incorporated into this agreement and are controlling.
perturbation.changed_text: 14.These restrictions may supersede, conflict with, or otherwise alter the employee obligations, rights, or liabilities created by Executive Order No. 12958, as amended; Section 7211 of Title 5, United States Code (governing disclosures to Congress); Section 1034 of Title 10, United States Code, as amended by the Military Whistleblower Protection Act (governing disclosure to Congress by members of the military); Section 2302(b)(8) of Title 5, United States Code, as amended by the Whistleblower Protection Act (governing disclosures of illegality, waste, fraud, abuse or public health or safety threats); the Intelligence Identities Protection Act of 1982 (50 USC 421 et seq.) (governing disclosures that could expose confidential Government agents); and the statutes which protect against disclosure that may compromise the national security, including Sections 641, 793, 794, 798, and 952 of Title 18, United States Code, and Section 4(b) of the Subversive Activities Act of 1950 (50 USC 783(b)). The definitions, requirements, obligations, rights, sanctions, and liabilities created by said Executive Order and listed statutes are not incorporated into this agreement and are not controlling.
perturbation.explanation: This change makes the non-disclosure agreement potentially superior to existing laws and executive orders, by stating the restrictions *may* supersede them. This creates an in-text contradiction. The contract attempts to create uncertainty about compliance with national security statutes and whistleblower protection acts. Also, by stating those definitions aren't incorporated, there is uncertainty on whether this non-disclosure agreement can even be enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15.Signing this Agreement does not bar disclosures to Congress or to an authorized official of an executive agency or the Department of Justice that are essential to reporting a substantial violation of law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 14 now states that the restrictions 'may supersede, conflict with, or otherwise alter' employee obligations and rights created by laws like the Whistleblower Protection Act. It also states that the definitions are 'not incorporated' and 'not controlling'. This directly contradicts section 15, which states that signing the agreement does not bar disclosures to Congress or authorized officials for reporting substantial violations of law. By suggesting the agreement *may* supersede whistleblower protections and not incorporating/controlling those protections, section 14 now conflicts with the guarantee of disclosure in section 15, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 16.I represent and warrant that I have the authority to enter into this Agreement. 17.I have read this Agreement carefully and my questions, if any, have been answered. I acknowledge that the briefing officer has made available to me any laws, regulations, or directives referenced in this document so that I may read them at this time, if I so choose.
perturbation.changed_text: 16.I represent and warrant that I may or may not have the authority to enter into this Agreement. 17. I may or may not have read this Agreement carefully and my questions, if any, may or may not have been answered. I acknowledge that the briefing officer may or may not have made available to me any laws, regulations, or directives referenced in this document.
perturbation.explanation: By introducing uncertainty about the signatory's authority to enter the agreement and whether the agreement was read and understood, this modification invalidates the entire premise of the contract. It creates a serious in-text contradiction as it questions the very foundation of the agreement. It also makes it uncertain whether the signatory has sufficient information for the contract to even take effect.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. I hereby acknowledge that I have received a security indoctrination concerning the nature and protection of information to which I have been provided conditional access, including the procedures to be followed in ascertaining whether other persons to whom I contemplate disclosing this information have been approved for access to it, and that I understand these procedures.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces uncertainty about the signatory's authority and understanding of the agreement, stating 'I may or may not have the authority' and 'I may or may not have read this Agreement carefully.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text which assumes the signatory understands and has received security indoctrination. If the signatory 'may not have read' the agreement, it casts doubt on whether they understand the procedures outlined in the contradicted text regarding the protection of information and access protocols, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The change invalidates the assumption that the signatory is informed, therefore I assign YES.
